title: REGIONAL STANDARD FOR CANNED FOUL MEDAMES 1 (Near East) 2

sections.section: REGIONAL STANDARD FOR CANNED FOUL MEDAMES 1 (Near East) 2
sections.summary: Document title only: Regional Standard for Canned Foul Medames (Near East).
States the Standard applies to Canned Foul Medames as defined in Section 2 and intended for direct consumption.

Defines Canned Foul Medames as cooked, soaked and heat-processed Vicia faba pulses packed in a well-sealed container and heat-processed to prevent spoilage, and includes the concept of 'squashable' (pulses softened by cooking with partial pulp breakdown).

Product definition reiterating that Canned Foul Medames are Vicia faba pulses prepared by soaking, heat treatment and canning (with optional ingredients), packed in a sealed container and heat-processed to prevent spoilage.
Defines 'squashable' as dry Vicia faba pulses cooked until soft, with some skins cracking and parts of the pulp disintegrating into the liquid.

Specifies essential composition and quality: the basic ingredient is Vicia faba pulses and optional ingredients (vegetable oil; salt max 2%; tomato sauce/garlic/spices max 2% single or 4% mixed; tehena; lemon juice); general quality requirements mandate ingredient compliance with standards, absence of abnormal flavour/odour/colour and foreign matter, and specific requirements set drained-weight minima (≥65% net weight without optionals, ≥67% with optionals), prohibit preservatives and colouring (except as noted), and require uniform colour/size and a tender texture.
Specifies the basic ingredient: pulses of Vicia faba.
Lists optional ingredients permitted in the product including vegetable oil; salt (max 2%); tomato sauce, garlic and spices (max 2% single, 4% when mixed); tehena; and lemon juice.
Optional ingredient: vegetable oil.

sections.section: 3.2.2 Salt in a maximum limit of 2%.
sections.summary: Optional ingredient: salt, limited to a maximum of 2%.

sections.section: 3.2.3 Tomato sauce, garlic and spices in a maximum limit of 2% single and of 4% when mixed.
sections.summary: Optional ingredients: tomato sauce, garlic and spices permitted with maximum limits of 2% if used singly and 4% when mixed.
Optional ingredient: Tehena.
Optional ingredient: lemon juice.

General quality factors require all ingredients (including additives) to comply with their standards and be safe for consumption, the product to be free from abnormal flavour/odour/colour and foreign matter, and free from insects.

sections.section: 3.3.1 All the ingredients, including food additives, shall comply with their own standards, and be safe for human consumption.
sections.summary: Obligation that all ingredients, including food additives, must comply with their respective standards and be safe for human consumption.

sections.section: 3.3.2 The product shall be free from abnormal flavour, odour, colour and have the characteristics typical of the final product.
sections.summary: Requires the product to be free from abnormal flavour, odour and colour and to possess the characteristics typical of the final product.

sections.section: 3.3.3 Product shall be free from insects and any foreign matter.
sections.summary: Requires the product to be free from insects and any foreign matter.

Sets specific quality requirements for the product when prepared ready-for-consumption, including drained weight minimums, prohibition of preservatives and colouring (except as allowed in Subclause 4.2), and requirements for uniform appearance and tender, distinguishable texture.

sections.section: 3.4.1 Drained weight shall be not less than 65% of the net weight, without the added optional ingredients.
sections.summary: Specifies a minimum drained weight of not less than 65% of the net weight, excluding optional ingredients.

sections.section: 3.4.2 Drained weight shall be not less than 67% of the net weight, with the added optional ingredients.
sections.summary: Specifies a minimum drained weight of not less than 67% of the net weight when optional ingredients are included.

sections.section: 3.4.3 The product shall not contain any preservative (Subclause 4.2 excluded) or colouring substance.
sections.summary: Prohibits any preservative (except as permitted under Subclause 4.2) and any colouring substance in the product.

sections.section: 3.4.4 Foul pulses shall match in colour, size and appearance. The texture of the product shall be distinguishable and tender.
sections.summary: Requires pulses to be uniform in colour, size and appearance, and for the product texture to be distinguishable and tender.
States that only the food additives listed in the following provisions may be used, and then only within the specified maximum levels.
Permits citric acid (INS 330) as an acidity regulator at GMP (good manufacturing practice) levels.
Permits EDTA compounds (INS 385, 386) as antioxidant/preservative up to 365 mg/kg (as anhydrous calcium disodium EDTA), singly or in combination.
Requires the product to comply with Codex maximum limits for contaminants and with Codex maximum residue limits for pesticides.

Recommends preparation and handling in accordance with relevant Codex hygienic codes (e.g., CXC 1-1969, CXC 23-1979) and requires compliance with any microbiological criteria established per CXG 21-1997.

Requires packaging that safeguards the hygienic, nutritional and technological quality and storage in well-ventilated, protected conditions; metal containers must be coated with suitable food-grade lacquer and be free of defects and rust, and sealed containers must show no swelling with internal pressure below atmospheric at 20°C.
Heading only — introduces the Weights and Measures section and contains no substantive requirements in this item.
Heading only — introduces the ‘Fill of the Container’ subsection and contains no substantive requirements in this item.

Requires the container to be filled so the product occupies at least 90% of the water capacity, defined as the volume of distilled water at 20°C that the sealed container will hold when completely filled.
Requires labelling to comply with the Codex General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985).
Specifies that the name of the food shall be “Foul Medames.”
Draft/editorial note only — methods of analysis and sampling are indicated as to be finalized.
